name: gpiozero (Raspberry Pi GPIO Python Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3); Ben Nuttall / Raspberry Pi Foundation

auth.notes: Hardware GPIO access; runs on Raspberry Pi

performance.notes: Hardware-bound; Python GPIO overhead ~1ms; not for hard real-time

agent_readiness.notes: High-level GPIO library for Raspberry Pi. from gpiozero import LED, Button, MotionSensor, Buzzer, PWMLED. led = LED(17). led.on(). led.off(). led.blink(on_time=0.5, off_time=0.5). button = Button(2). button.when_pressed = led.on. button.when_released = led.off. PIR sensor: pir = MotionSensor(4). servo = Servo(18). Distance: DistanceSensor(echo=17, trigger=4). Remote GPIO over network. Mock pins for testing without hardware. Named devices: TrafficHat, LED strips. Pre-installed on Raspberry Pi OS.

gotchas: Raspberry Pi hardware required (or mock pins for testing)
Not for hard real-time (Python GIL, ~1ms jitter)
GPIO pin numbering: BCM (default) vs BOARD mode
best_when: Raspberry Pi GPIO prototyping and education
LED, button, sensor, servo, and motor control
avoid_when: Non-Raspberry Pi Linux boards (use lgpio directly)
summary: gpiozero is Raspberry Pi's high-level GPIO library. LED(17).on(), LED(17).blink(). Button(2).when_pressed = callback. MotionSensor(4), Servo(18), DistanceSensor(echo, trigger). Event-driven GPIO with callbacks. Mock pins for testing without hardware. Remote GPIO over network. Pre-installed on Raspberry Pi OS. BSD-3. Best Raspberry Pi GPIO library — simple, Pythonic, and well-documented. For hard real-time use C or RTOS.
